---
title: "Data_managment"
author: "Arturo Bertero"
date: "2024-10-17"
output: html_document
---

# Input

```{r}
#Libraries
library(pacman)
p_load(lavaan, rio, tidyverse, psych, here, stargazer, summarytools, visdat,
       EGAnet, qgraph, semPlot, haven, janitor, readxl, labelled, countrycode,
       openxlsx)

#Options
rm(list = ls())
options(scipen = 99)
```

```{r}
#Data SUSPECT
SUSPECTS_GCB5 = read_rds(here("Input", "SUSPECTS.rds")) 

# Data CCRS
CCRS = read_csv(here("Input", "CCRS", "CCRS.csv")) %>% clean_names()

# Enders et al
# We use the Qualtrics 2021 dataset as it has the richest set of CBs (17 items) + the 4 ACTS items
Study2_ACTS <- read_dta(here("Input", "Enders", "Qualtrics 2021.dta")) %>% 
  clean_names()
```


# Processing

## SUSPECTS

```{r}
# SUSPECTS is clean: right names and no NAs
names(SUSPECTS_GCB5)
summary(is.na.data.frame(SUSPECTS_GCB5))
```

## Enders

```{r}
# Clean study 2 (ACTS)
Study2_ACTS = Study2_ACTS %>% 
  # country label
  mutate(country = "USA") %>%
  
  # Rename the 4 CM items
  rename(
    cm_1 = con1, 
    cm_2 = con2, 
    cm_3 = con3, 
    cm_4 = con4,
    
    # Rename ONLY the 6 CB items that are on the 1-5 scale (11 are removed)
    cb_1 = epstein, 
    cb_2 = lightbulbs, 
    cb_3 = fda, 
    cb_4 = soros, 
    cb_5 = billgates, 
    cb_6 = cellphone
  ) %>%
  
  # Select only the country and the renamed items, then drop NAs
  select(country, starts_with("cm_"), starts_with("cb_")) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  
  # Calculate CM and CB indices 
  mutate(
    CM = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("cm_"))),
    CB = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("cb_")))
  ) %>%
  
  # Reorder cols
  select(country, CM, CB, starts_with("cm_"), starts_with("cb_"))

# Verify 
map(Study2_ACTS, summary)

```


## CCRS

```{r}
# 1, Base: cb items asked in all countries and CB index
CCRS_base <- CCRS %>%
  mutate(
    country = id_q2,
    # Recompute CB index using ONLY the 4 items asked in all countries
    CB = rowMeans(select(., id_q36_1:id_q36_4), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  rename(
    cb_1 = id_q36_1, 
    cb_2 = id_q36_2, 
    cb_3 = id_q36_3, 
    cb_4 = id_q36_4
  )

# 2, Dataset A: Bruder et al. CMQ (5 items)
Study3_CMQ <- CCRS_base %>%
  rename(
    CM = id_q33,
    cm_1 = id_q33_1, cm_2 = id_q33_2, cm_3 = id_q33_3,
    cm_4 = id_q33_4, cm_5 = id_q33_5
  ) %>%
  select(country, CM, CB, starts_with("cm_"), starts_with("cb_")) %>%
  na.omit() 

# Clean up environment
remove(CCRS_base)

# Verify the remaining Study 3 dataset
map(Study3_CMQ, summary)
```


# Output

```{r}
# List  
master_datasets <- list(
  Study1_SUSPECTS = SUSPECTS_GCB5,
  Study2_ACTS     = Study2_ACTS,
  Study3_CMQ      = Study3_CMQ
)

# Save 
saveRDS(master_datasets, here("Input", "Secondary", "master_datasets.rds"))

```
